title: NoSQL operations should not be vulnerable to injection attacks
description: NoSQL injections occur when an application retrieves untrusted data and inserts it into a database query without sanitizing it first. What is the potential impact? In the context of a web application that is vulnerable to NoSQL injection: After discovering the injection point, attackers insert data into the vulnerable field to execute malicious commands in the affected databases. Below are some real-world scenarios that illustrate some impacts of an attacker exploiting the vulnerability.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.triggering_conditions: MethodDeclaration nodes that contain NoSQL query strings using string interpolation or concatenation with variables
logic.reasoning: Detects NoSQL injection vulnerabilities where query strings use string interpolation or concatenation with variables. Identifies patterns like string interpolation in SELECT statements, MongoDB filter objects, and WHERE clauses. Excludes safe patterns like query builders, parameterized queries, and non-query contexts like logging.

should_trigger: var query = $"SELECT * FROM c WHERE c.id = '{id}'";
var query = $"SELECT * FROM c WHERE c.category = '{category}'"
should_not_trigger: new SqlQuerySpec("SELECT * FROM c WHERE c.id = @id", params);
[Test] public void Test() { var query = $"SELECT * FROM c WHERE c.id = '{'test'}'"; }
